features.name: Agent Configuration UI
claimed_atoms: Agent configuration create/edit dialog exists with name, prompt, model selection, provider config, using electron-store

observations: AgentDef type defined locally with id, name, prompt, description, model, variant, temperature, top_p, color, steps
loads custom agents from IPC via typed api() accessor on mount with loaded() guard
loads built-in agents from sync.data.agent filtering out subagent/hidden modes
merges both lists in createMemo, grouped by source (built-in/custom)
search enabled on List component with filterKeys: name, description, model, builtinModel
create opens DialogEditAgent via dialog.show()
edit/delete buttons shown only for custom agents
saveCustomAgents calls both local state setter and IPC setCustomAgents
deletion without confirmation prompt — instant removal with toast

Form fields: name (TextField, autofocus), prompt (multiline TextField, monospace), description, model (Select), variant, temperature (number min=0 max=2 step=0.1), top_p (number min=0 max=1 step=0.05), color (15 color swatches)
Model options from useProviders().connected() — each provider's models flattened
Model select wrapped in <Show when={modelOptions().length > 0}> — hidden when no providers connected
handleSave constructs AgentDef with crypto.randomUUID() for new agents
Save button disabled when name is empty
temperature and top_p parseFloat with NaN guard
Validates and creates proper AgentDef structure
Properly exported as Component
Uses same padding/sticky header layout as other settings panels
Contains single Manage Agents button that opens DialogManageAgents
Imports and uses DialogManageAgents correctly
Adds two handlers at end of registerIpcHandlers: get-desktop-custom-agents and set-desktop-custom-agents
Uses getStore('desktop-custom-agents') to access dedicated electron-store
Store key is 'agents' for the custom agents array
get handler returns (store.get('agents') as unknown[]) ?? [] — handles missing key
Both handlers are additive (11 new lines, 0 existing modified)
Pre-existing import './install-cli' (line 15) causes type error — but this is from another lane and predates our feature
AgentDef type added with id, name, prompt, description?, model?, variant?, temperature?, top_p?, color?, steps?
ElectronAPI gains getCustomAgents: () => Promise<AgentDef[]> and setCustomAgents: (agents: AgentDef[]) => Promise<void>
Also includes getDesktopPluginConfig? from another lane (optional)
All changes are additive (16 new lines)
getCustomAgents wired to ipcRenderer.invoke('get-desktop-custom-agents')
setCustomAgents wired to ipcRenderer.invoke('set-desktop-custom-agents', agents)
Also includes getDesktopPluginConfig from another lane
All changes are additive (3 new lines)
Imports SettingsAgents from ./settings-agents (additive, 1 line)
Agents tab trigger added between Shortcuts and Providers (additive, 4 lines)
Agents tab content added after Models content (additive, 3 lines)
Total: 8 lines added, 0 removed — fully additive
Icon: brain, value: 'agents'
Window interface declaration extended with getCustomAgents and setCustomAgents on api property
Uses unknown[] type (not AgentDef) — consistent with dialog-manage-agents typed accessor
Changes are additive (2 lines)
All agent dialog keys present: manage, manage.description, create, edit, name, prompt, model, variant, temperature, top_p, color, delete, delete.confirm, source.builtin, source.custom
Settings keys: settings.agents.title, settings.agents.description

FINDING: key 'dialog.agents.description' (used in dialog-edit-agent.tsx line 103 for the description field label) is NOT defined — closest key is 'dialog.agents.manage.description' which is semantically different

boundary_probes.scenario: electron-store has no 'agents' key yet (first launch)
boundary_probes.expected: Empty array returned, UI shows no custom agents
boundary_probes.actual: ipc handler returns (store.get('agents') as unknown[]) ?? [] — ?? [] ensures empty array fallback. Component renders no custom agents gracefully.

boundary_probes.expected: Empty built-in agents list, UI still works
boundary_probes.actual: createMemo uses (sync.data.agent ?? []).filter(...) — ?? [] handles undefined. Empty list renders fallback message.

boundary_probes.scenario: No providers connected when editing model
boundary_probes.actual: Wrapped in <Show when={modelOptions().length > 0}> — hidden when no options available.

boundary_probes.scenario: Type consistency between ElectronAPI and component api() accessor
boundary_probes.actual: IPC returns (store.get('agents') as unknown[]). Component casts to AgentDef[]. No runtime validation — type safety gap but runtime safe.

findings.description: Missing i18n key: 'dialog.agents.description' is used in dialog-edit-agent.tsx line 103 for the description field label, but not defined in i18n/en.ts. The translation system will likely render a missing-key fallback or the raw key string.
findings.recommendation: Add '"dialog.agents.description": "Description"' to i18n/en.ts in the dialog.agents section

features.name: Context Display Enhancement
claimed_atoms: Context display indicator shows compaction events and warning color at 80%+ threshold

observations: Usage percentage tracked via createMemo: `context()?.usage ?? 0`
hasCompaction memo iterates messages checking for parts of type 'compaction'
usageColor memo returns CSS variable for danger (>95%) or warning (>=80%) or undefined (<80%)
usageColor applied to wrapper div via --border-active CSS variable on ProgressCircle
Compaction badge: absolute-positioned 2px warning dot at top-right of the indicator
Compaction badge tooltip shows 'Compacted' label with collapse icon
context(), cost, hasCompaction all shown in tooltip
Re-uses existing getSessionContextMetrics function

boundary_probes.scenario: Usage is null (session with no messages)
boundary_probes.actual: context()?.usage ?? 0 — optional chaining handles null. usageColor returns undefined for <80%.
Code checks pct > 95 (strictly greater than). At exactly 95%, code falls through to pct >= 80 check, showing yellow (warning) instead of red (danger).
hasCompaction returns false, badge not rendered. Correct.
Wrapped in <Show when={params.id}> — entire component hidden when no session ID.

findings.description: Threshold mismatch: plan specifies 'red >= 95%' but code uses `pct > 95`. At exactly 95% usage, the indicator shows warning (yellow) instead of danger (red). The 0.5% difference (<1 token) is negligible in practice.
findings.recommendation: Change `pct > 95` to `pct >= 95` in the usageColor memo to match plan specification exactly

findings.description: Unused import: `createSignal` imported from solid-js (line 1) but never used in the component body.
findings.recommendation: Remove `createSignal` from the import to keep imports clean

features.name: Settings Polish
claimed_atoms: Settings dialog has reorganized categories with Agents tab, search in panels, and improved keyboard nav

observations: Import of SettingsAgents added (1 line, additive)
Agents tab trigger (value='agents', icon='brain') added between Shortcuts and Providers (additive, 4 lines)
Agents tab content added after Models (additive, 3 lines)
No existing code removed or restructured — fully additive change (8 lines added)
Uses existing Tabs component with vertical orientation and settings variant
Keyboard nav already provided by Kobalte Tabs (pre-existing, not part of this change)

cross_cutting_observations.description: No new type errors introduced by feature code in either packages/app or packages/desktop.
cross_cutting_observations.details: packages/app: 5 pre-existing errors (session-viz, onboarding — other lanes). packages/desktop: 1 pre-existing error (./install-cli not found — other lane). Zero errors in new or modified feature files.

cross_cutting_observations.description: packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts line 15: Cannot find module './install-cli'. This is from a prior lane's install-cli feature. The import was not cleaned up when install-cli.ts was removed or moved. This error pre-dates our feature changes and is not caused by our additive handlers.

non_blocking_findings.summary: Usage threshold mismatch: code uses pct > 95 (strict) vs plan spec >= 95

deferred_seams: No runtime validation that stored custom agents match AgentDef shape
delete confirmation dialog not implemented for custom agents (immediate delete with toast only)
